title: Harden connector 502 transport observability
goal: Make intermittent ChatGPT-to-Grabowski connector 502 and stream/timeout failures diagnosable and less likely by adding a bounded transport-failure classification, narrow probe guidance and retry/splitting rules without treating retries as proof of correctness.

acceptance.assertion: Friction classification distinguishes connector transport 502/stream exceptions from runtime policy gates, command return codes, platform filters and repository failures.
A documented or typed probe captures operator/tunnel service state, recent streamable_http errors, request concurrency hints and adjacent successful calls using bounded, redacted output.
Operator docs define when to split broad terminal calls into smaller typed calls, when to retry once, and when to stop and record friction instead of looping.
A successful retry never proves the first failure was harmless; status output preserves non-claims and recommends follow-up only when recurrence threshold is met.
Focused tests cover classification of 502-like connector symptoms, timeout symptoms, adjacent successful retry, and non-confusion with fail-closed recovery gates.

observed_symptoms: grabowski_terminal_run broad combined grep/find command returned 502 upstream/external service error while a smaller follow-up call succeeded.
grabowski_recovery_status initially timed out and then succeeded on retry with all recovery checks green.
metadata.live_basis: grabowski-operator.service and tunnel-client-grabowski.service were active with zero restarts; journal near the event showed streamable_http POST handling exceptions and many concurrent forwarded commands.
